response.justification: The candidate is a deeply experienced individual contributor with genuine strengths in testing infrastructure, developer tooling, and CI/CD — well aligned with the engineering-productivity domain — but this is a Level II people-management role, and the resume shows no formal people-management or team-leadership experience. There is no evidence of running one-on-ones, performance management, or organizational roadmap ownership, which are the core responsibilities here. Strong technical fit for a senior IC role on this team, but not for the manager position as scoped.
strengths: Extensive hands-on experience with testing tools and infrastructure (test runners, assertion libraries, Selenium training, CI server setup)
Long, broad software development career (well over the 8-year minimum) spanning web, mobile, and low-latency systems
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and mentoring instincts through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: No formal people-management experience — the role's 2-year management minimum is not met
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or setting team-level strategy and roadmaps
Developer-tools work is largely personal/open-source projects rather than sustained professional ownership of build or release systems at scale

key_factors.factor: Mobile platform exposure (iOS, Apple TV, Swift)
